title: Component Architecture

questions.question_text: What is the primary way to pass data from a parent component to a child component in React?
questions.explanation: Props (short for properties) are the standard way to pass data down the component tree.

questions.question_text: What is the primary benefit of 'Component-Based Architecture'?
options: It makes the code run faster
It allows you to build complex UIs by composing small, isolated, and reusable pieces of code
It eliminates the need for CSS
questions.explanation: Components promote reusability and make large codebases easier to maintain.

options: External inputs passed to a component from its parent
A way to store data in a database
The CSS styles applied to a component
questions.explanation: Props are read-only to the child component and allow parent-to-child communication.

options: A syntax extension for JavaScript that looks like HTML
questions.explanation: JSX allows you to write the UI structure directly within your JavaScript code.

questions.question_text: Which rule is mandatory for React component names?
options: They must start with an underscore
They must start with an Uppercase letter
They must be shorter than 10 characters
questions.explanation: React uses the uppercase first letter to distinguish between custom components and standard HTML tags.

options: A lightweight, in-memory copy of the real DOM used by React for performance optimization
questions.explanation: React compares the Virtual DOM with the real DOM and only updates the parts that changed (Reconciliation).

questions.question_text: How do you pass a value named 'title' into a component named 'Header'?
questions.explanation: Props are passed like HTML attributes.

questions.question_text: What does it mean that React data flow is 'Unidirectional'?
options: Data can only be changed by the user
Data flows only from parent to child via props
Data can only move horizontally between siblings
questions.explanation: This makes behavior more predictable and easier to debug.

options: Moving state from a parent to a child
Moving state from a child to its nearest common ancestor so multiple children can share it
questions.explanation: This is the standard way to share data between sibling components in React.

questions.question_text: In JSX, how do you embed a variable named 'username'?
questions.explanation: Curly braces {} are used to execute JavaScript logic inside JSX.

options: To group multiple elements without adding an extra unnecessary <div> to the DOM
questions.explanation: Fragments keep the DOM tree cleaner.

questions.question_text: True or False: A React component can return multiple sibling elements without a wrapper.
questions.explanation: A component must return a single root element (or a Fragment).

options: Using 'children' or specific props to pass components into other components
questions.explanation: Composition is preferred over inheritance for building flexible UI components.

questions.question_text: How do you access the content between the opening and closing tags of a component?
questions.explanation: The 'children' prop is automatically populated with nested content.

questions.question_text: Which of these is a functional component?
questions.explanation: Functional components are now the standard in modern React development.

questions.question_text: What is the difference between a Component and an Element in React?
options: A Component is a blueprint (function); an Element is a specific instance (returned object)
Components are in HTML, Elements are in JS
Elements are only for the backend
questions.explanation: Calling a component function produces a React element.
